_meta.purpose: Statewide CS associate-degree templates (spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-as-degree-data-design.md §1A). Two templates: cs_local (fitted from 45 local CS A.S. degrees) and cs_ast (the CS Transfer Model Curriculum). Concept slots reference the prereq_concept vocabulary. Imported by scripts/import_as_degrees.py.
_meta.method: Fitted from the statewide extraction (scripts/data/as_degrees_cs_extraction.json) by concept frequency, mapping courses to concepts BY TITLE (not prefix — some colleges teach CS under MATH/other prefixes). Thresholds: >=70% of degrees -> required core; 40-70% -> common (choose/optional); <40% -> optional, noted only. Local-GE modeled as Title 5 areas; AS-T GE is the Cal-GETC pattern.
_meta.review: Approved by Tybalt at the G2 gate (2026-07-17, 'looks fine, proceed') covering docs/as-degree-template-review.md — the two-template approach and the fitted concept slots below.

templates.name: Associate of Science — Computer Science (statewide local template)
templates.note: Fitted from 45 local CS A.S. degrees. Looser than the AS-T: a stable programming+architecture+discrete core with variable math depth and mostly-optional science. GE is the local Title 5 pattern, not IGETC.

groups.label: Programming core
groups.note: cs_1 at 97%. A second programming/OOP or data-structures course is common (cs_2_oop 48%, cs_3_data_structures 62%) — modeled as a required intro plus a choose-one advanced slot.

groups.label: Calculus
groups.note: calc_1 62%, calc_2 46%. Calc I required; Calc II common (choose-to-depth).

groups.label: Science (optional at most colleges)
groups.note: phys_mech 31%, gen_chem_1 13% — present at a minority; modeled as an optional choose-one.

groups.label: General education (local Title 5 pattern)
groups.note: Local associate-degree GE (natural sciences, social/behavioral, humanities, language & rationality, math competency); not enumerated per college.

templates.name: Associate in Science in Computer Science for Transfer (TMC)
templates.note: The statewide CS Transfer Model Curriculum. Tight and uniform across colleges. Required core = programming + discrete + architecture + Calc I/II + calculus-based physics; plus one List-B science (biology / chemistry / physics E&M). GE is the Cal-GETC pattern.

groups.label: Programming sequence
groups.note: cs_1 87%; a second programming/data-structures course is part of the TMC sequence (cs_2_oop 28% + cs_3_data_structures 51% — same slot, classifier split).

groups.label: Calculus I and II
groups.note: calc_1 98%, calc_2 88% — both required in the TMC.

groups.label: List B science (choose one)
groups.note: TMC List B: one additional science — biology (50%), chemistry (38%), or physics E&M (22%).

groups.label: General education (Cal-GETC)
groups.note: AS-T degrees complete Cal-GETC; not enumerated.
